name: v2-architecture-decisions
description: Load-bearing decisions made during V2 design — two-tier execution, closed contributes namespace, FSEvents-first, no daemon tier, native-only Tier 3.
body_preview:
## V2 architecture — load-bearing decisions

### Two execution tiers (no daemon)

V2 has **native** plugins (Swift code compiled into host binary) and **script** plugins (executables that print JSON). NO daemon execution model in V1 of V2.

**Why:** with no external plugin authors and native plugins

name: v2-platform-migration
description: claude-instances V2 is a plugin platform rewrite running in a parallel worktree, with V1 preserved on legacy/v1 branch for fallback. Live dual-binary during transition.
body_preview:
## V2 platform migration — high-level context

Started 2026-05-15. V2 turns the existing macOS menu-bar app into a plugin platform: every dashboard tab, menubar entry, status badge, etc. becomes a plugin (native Swift or script). Driven by user's stated goal of making claude-instances "the central p

name: Codebase Convention Compliance
description: Rules about scanning existing patterns, module placement, naming, and auth design before writing new code
body_preview:
## Scan existing codebase patterns before writing new code

Before adding new code to an existing project, scan for established patterns — config loading, env variable access, auth modules, naming conventions — and follow them. Do not write idiomatic code that ignores the project's conventions.

**W

name: client-env-exposure-pushback
description: Proactively flag when env vars are proposed with NEXT_PUBLIC_ or equivalent client-bundle exposure when they don't need to be client-visible
body_preview:
## Push back on unnecessary client-bundle exposure of env vars

When the user (or a design being reviewed) marks an env var as client-visible (e.g. `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix in Next.js, `VITE_` in Vite, etc.), proactively ask whether the value actually needs to be in the client bundle. If it's used only

name: structural-deletion-without-investigation
description: Never delete or replace a structural component pattern before understanding the architectural invariant it enforces
body_preview:
## Do not delete or replace structural components without first understanding why they exist

Before removing or consolidating a component that has an architectural split (e.g. a server/client pair, a sync/async variant, a base/derived pair), investigate the reason the split was introduced. The spli

name: Worker overhaul — active todo pointer

body_preview:
The worker-overhaul session has a running todo list across three places
that MUST stay in sync after every turn that touches tasks:

1. **Claude Code TaskList** (authoritative runtime state)
2. **Written notes** — `frontend/.claude/notes/worker-overhaul-todos.md`
3. **This memory pointer** (you're r

name: Environment Variable Conventions
description: Frontend vs backend boolean semantics and other env var conventions this user enforces
body_preview:
## Frontend env booleans use true/false; backend env booleans use 1/0

When checking an environment variable as a boolean flag: frontend (Next.js, browser-side, Vercel) code uses `=== "true"` / `=== "false"` string comparison. Backend (Python, Rust, server processes) code uses `== "1"` / `== "0"`.

name: Session Continuity Patterns
description: User relies heavily on /catchup and /core-dump for long sessions; compaction hits frequently on complex tasks
body_preview:
User runs very long sessions with frequent context compaction. They rely on `/catchup` and `/core-dump` to resume work across compaction boundaries.

**Why:** Sessions like the dream dashboard work had 70+ tool calls in a single turn and 25+ conversation turns — far past typical context limits. The

name: Architecture Anti-Patterns and Opt-In Design
description: Flag-driven errors, optional local-dev integrations, opt-in behavioral features
body_preview:
## Error classification must be flag-driven, not string-driven

Never branch on `error.message` string content to decide rendering, routing, or behavior. Attach a stable `code` field (enum/union) to errors at the producer and switch on that at the consumer.

**Why:** Regex/substring matching on erro

name: Logger session scope — ASK before picking up worker/jobs FE
description: When resuming the logger workstream, default to logger-domain work but ASK the user before refusing cross-session jobs/worker requests — they can and do explicitly invite cross-session work, especially for visual fixes that span both surfaces.
body_preview: **Updated 2026-05-09:** Earlier in this session I refused to touch jobs/worker FE files (force-status reverse, worker-status nav dropdown) citing this scoping rule. The user explicitly authorized them anyway and was mildly annoyed that I asked instead of just doing the small fixes. The rule below is

- [Consistent env var naming across snippets](feedback_consistent_env_var_names.md) — use LOGGER_CRAB_TOKEN_FULL/PUBLIC in all cu

name: stick-with-one-env-var-naming-across-snippets
description: When proposing shell snippets and scripts that use the same env vars, use one canonical name throughout — switching between short (TF, TP) and long (LOGGER_CRAB_TOKEN_FULL) names mid-conversation causes the user to copy-paste broken commands
body_preview: When generating example shell snippets and a final script that operate on the same env vars, **use one canonical name across all snippets and the final artifact**. Don't introduce short aliases like `TF`/`TP` in inline examples and then switch to `LOGGER_CRAB_TOKEN_FULL`/`LOGGER_CRAB_TOKEN_PUBLIC` i

name: render-internal-port-is-10000
description: Render Web Services internally expose port 10000, not whatever PORT is set in render.yaml — confirmed by the dashboard's Internal Address field
body_preview:
Render Web Services on this account/workspace bind to port **10000** internally, regardless of `PORT` in `render.yaml`. Render appears to override the env var at runtime — the YAML value is informational only.

**Internal URL for logger-crab from other Render services in same workspace:**
`http://lo

name: Verify the auth stack before claiming cost or coverage
description: Before reasoning about session-read latency, "JWT-only" claims, or where auth is actually enforced — read proxy.ts, db-permission.utils.ts, and session.db.ts in full. The auth path here is hybrid (JWT cookie + DB-backed accessToken via unstable_cache).
body_preview:
Before claiming "X is the authority on auth" / "JWT verify is the only cost" / "this layer is JWT-only" / "we need to add a cache here" for any auth path — read these files first (FE + BE):

**Frontend:**
- `frontend/src/proxy.ts` — middleware. JWT-only via `getToken()`. DB validation block is comme

name: Don't reach for dynamic require + try/catch — read the file first
description: When unsure whether a module is safe to import from a client-loaded context, READ the module. Most "config" / "utils" modules are just env-var reads with safe defaults and import cleanly on both runtimes. Dynamic require + try/catch is for ACTUAL server-only modules (DB drivers, node:fs, node:async_hooks).

name: vitest directory imports — use ./path/index, not ./path
description: vitest's resolver doesn't pick up `index.ts` from a directory import. `from "./logger"` returns a module with undefined named exports; `from "./logger/index"` works. Affects test files AND production source if it gets imported by a test.
body_preview: **Symptom (verified 2026-05-08):** `from "./logger"` in a test file (or in production code that gets imported by a test) silently returns a module with all named exports as `undefined` under vitest. `TypeError: __clearSinks is not a function` or `withLoggerContext is not a function` at runtime, even

name: Test files — sibling `*.test.ts` or `test/` folder, never `__tests__/`
description: This repo's convention is sibling `foo.test.ts` next to `foo.ts`, OR a `test/` folder. Never `__tests__/`. Vitest config (`include: ["src/**/*.test.ts"]`) picks up either; the convention difference is purely stylistic but the user has corrected this twice.
body_preview:
**Repo convention (verified 2026-05-07):**

- Default: sibling `foo.test.ts` next to `foo.ts`
  - Example: `src/utils/hooks/use-query-sync/core.test.ts` next to `core.ts`
- When grouping multiple test files: `test/` folder
  - Example: `src/utils/logger/test/logger.demo.test.ts`
- **Never use `__tes

name: Maximalist ≠ ambitious — agents fail at "tower without a spine
description: Maximalist scope (wide but flat — many parallel small things) works for agents. Ambitious scope (tall stack of dependent layers) doesn't, because hallucination compounds across levels.
body_preview:
**Direct quote from user (2026-05-07):**
> "Maximalist is different from ambitious, yes. In my experience, ambitious
> does not work with agents since there's hallucination and a tower without
> a spine."

## The distinction

| | MAXIMALIST | AMBITIOUS |
|---|---|---|
| Shape | Wide, flat — many par
